1	\chapter{{\v C}ech computation of higher direct images $R^i f_*$ (unconditional)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean
4	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
5	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
6	% (Lemmas \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
7	% \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},
8	% \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent},
9	% \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application},
10	% \ref{lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology}) (read from
11	% references/stacks-coherent.tex).
12	
13	\section{Setup and motivation}
14	
15	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
16	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
17	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
18	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
19	derived functor, which in a formalisation requires the ambient category of
20	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives; that property is not currently
21	available for sheaves of modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies
22	over the site. The {\v C}ech approach developed here sidesteps the issue entirely:
23	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
24	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
25	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
26	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
27	
28	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
29	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
30	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
31	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
32	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
33	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
34	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
35	\[
36	  \begin{array}{ccc}
37	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
38	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
39	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
40	  \end{array}
41	\]
42	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
43	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
44	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
45	
46	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
47	
48	\begin{definition}
49	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
50	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
52	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
53	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
54	  % \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
55	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
56	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
57	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
58	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
59	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
60	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
61	  % the unit ideal, see
62	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
63	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
64	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
65	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
66	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
67	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
68	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
69	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
70	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
71	  simplicial object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in simplicial degree
72	  \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
73	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
74	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
75	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
76	  \[
77	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
78	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
79	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
80	  \]
81	  with faces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and degeneracies by
82	  the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree \(-1\) is the object
83	  on all of \(X\). When \(X\) is separated each intersection
84	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the
85	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open
86	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in A\)
87	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
88	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).
89	\end{definition}
90	
91	\begin{definition}
92	[{\v C}ech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf]
93	  \label{def:cech_complex}
94	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex}
95	  \uses{def:cech_nerve}
96	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
97	  % \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof)
98	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L59--77).
99	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
100	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
101	  % is identified with the complex
102	  % $$
103	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
104	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
105	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
106	  % \ldots
107	  % $$
108	  % We are asked to show that the extended complex
109	  % \begin{equation}
110	  % \label{equation-extended}
111	  % 0 \to
112	  % M \to
113	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
114	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
115	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
116	  % \ldots
117	  % \end{equation}
118	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
119	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact."
120	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
121	  \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}.}
122	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
123	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with
124	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) affine (e.g.\ \(X\) separated). The
125	  \emph{{\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
126	  is the cochain complex obtained by applying the global-sections functor to the
127	  {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\);
128	  in degree \(p\) it is
129	  \[
130	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
131	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
132	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
133	  \]
134	  with differential \(d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p+1}\) the
135	  alternating sum \((d s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
136	  s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}\) of the
137	  restriction maps. Over an affine \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) with
138	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) and a standard cover by the
139	  \(D(f_i)\), this is the complex
140	  \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to \cdots\) of
141	  localisations. The \emph{relative {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet
142	  (\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) for \(f : X \to S\) is the analogous complex in
143	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) obtained by applying \(f_*\) over each finite
144	  intersection instead of global sections; its terms are
145	  \(\prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*
146	  \bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr)\), and it is a complex in
147	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) because each \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine and the
148	  pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated
149	  morphism is quasi-coherent.
150	\end{definition}
151	
152	\section{Affine acyclicity of the {\v C}ech complex}
153	
154	\begin{lemma}
155	[{\v C}ech acyclicity on affines]
156	  \label{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
157	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine}
158	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
159	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG (affine vanishing);
160	  % Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover
161	  % {\v C}ech vanishing) and Lemma
162	  % \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (Serre vanishing on affines)
163	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L44--52 and L145--155).
164	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
165	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
166	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$ be a standard
167	  % open covering of an affine open of $X$.
168	  % Then $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for
169	  % all $p > 0$."
170	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Serre vanishing: Higher cohomology vanishes on affine schemes
171	  % for quasi-coherent modules.
172	  % Let $X$ be a scheme.
173	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
174	  % For any affine open $U \subset X$ we have
175	  % $H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for all $p > 0$."
176	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemmas
177	  \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} and
178	  \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (Serre vanishing on affines;
179	  Tag 02KG).}
180	  Let \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) be an affine scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
181	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module, and
182	  \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) a standard open cover (so the
183	  \(f_i \in A\) generate the unit ideal). Then the {\v C}ech complex
184	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) has vanishing cohomology
185	  in all positive degrees:
186	  \[
187	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0,
188	  \]
189	  and consequently \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\).
190	\end{lemma}
191	
192	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Write $U = \Spec(A)$ for some ring $A$.
193	% In other words, $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ are elements of $A$
194	% which generate the unit ideal of $A$.
195	% Write $\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}$ for some $A$-module $M$.
196	% ...
197	% It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
198	% is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
199	% Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}.
200	% In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
201	% at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
202	% There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
203	% Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. ...
204	% Let us define a homotopy
205	% $$
206	% h :
207	% \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}}
208	% M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}
209	% \longrightarrow
210	% \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p}
211	% M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_p}, \mathfrak p}
212	% $$
213	% by the rule
214	% $$
215	% h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}
216	% $$
217	% ... We compute ... $(dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ ...
218	% This proves the identity map is homotopic to zero as desired."
219	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We are going to apply
220	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-vanish-basis}.
221	% As our basis $\mathcal{B}$ for the topology of $X$ we are going to use
222	% the affine opens of $X$. ... Note that the intersection of standard opens
223	% in an affine is another standard open. Hence property (1) holds. ...
224	% Finally, condition (3) of the lemma follows from
225	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}."
226	\begin{proof}
227	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
228	  Write \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) for an
229	  \(A\)-module \(M\). By Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} the {\v C}ech complex of
230	  the standard cover is the complex of localisations
231	  \[
232	    \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \;\to\;
233	    \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \;\to\;
234	    \prod_{i_0 i_1 i_2} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1} f_{i_2}} \;\to\; \cdots,
235	  \]
236	  and the claim \(\check{H}^p = 0\) for \(p > 0\) is equivalent to exactness of the
237	  extended complex \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \cdots\). Exactness may
238	  be checked after localising at an arbitrary prime \(\mathfrak{p} \subset A\).
239	  Choose an index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) with
240	  \(f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \notin \mathfrak{p}\); then
241	  \(M_{f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}, \mathfrak{p}} = M_{\mathfrak{p}}\), and the
242	  prescription \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\)
243	  defines a contracting homotopy of the localised extended complex: a direct
244	  computation gives \((dh + hd)(s) = s\), so the identity is homotopic to zero and
245	  the localised complex is exact. As exactness holds after every localisation, the
246	  extended complex is exact and \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for
247	  \(p > 0\).
248	
249	  For the sheaf-cohomology statement, take the affine opens of \(U\) as a basis of
250	  the topology and the standard open covers as the admissible covers. Intersections
251	  of standard opens are again standard opens, standard covers are cofinal among
252	  covers of a basic open, and the {\v C}ech vanishing just proved supplies the
253	  acyclicity hypothesis; the {\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis then
254	  yields \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\). \emph{(This is Serre
255	  vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affine schemes.)}
256	\end{proof}

258	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
259	
260	\begin{lemma}
261	[{\v C}ech complex computes the higher direct images]
262	  \label{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
263	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage}
264	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, def:cech_complex}
265	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KE; Lemma
266	  % \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent} ({\v C}ech computes cohomology when
267	  % all intersections are affine) and Lemma
268	  % \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}
269	  % ($H^q(X,\mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F})$ for affine $S$)
270	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L245--256 and L843--854).
271	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
272	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ be an open covering such that
273	  % $U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ is affine open for all $p \ge 0$ and all
274	  % $i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I$.
275	  % In this case for any quasi-coherent sheaf $\mathcal{F}$ we have
276	  % $$
277	  % \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})
278	  % $$
279	  % as $\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$-modules for all $p$."
280	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
281	  % Assume that $f$ is quasi-separated and quasi-compact.
282	  % Assume $S$ is affine.
283	  % For any quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{F}$
284	  % we have
285	  % $$
286	  % H^q(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})
287	  % $$
288	  % for all $q \in \mathbf{Z}$."
289	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemmas
290	  \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent} and
291	  \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application} (Tag 02KE).}
292	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism of schemes, let
293	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let
294	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover of
295	  \(X\) (so, by separatedness, every intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is
296	  affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative {\v C}ech complex
297	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
298	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} compute the higher direct images: for every
299	  \(i \geq 0\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules
300	  \[
301	    H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
302	      \;\cong\; R^i f_*\mathcal{F}.
303	  \]
304	  In particular, over an affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), taking global
305	  sections gives \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
306	  H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\) as \(A\)-modules for all \(i\).
307	\end{lemma}

310	% Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}
311	% this is a special case of
312	% Cohomology, Lemma
313	% \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-spectral-sequence-application}."
314	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Consider the Leray spectral sequence
315	% $E_2^{p, q} = H^p(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})$
316	% converging to $H^{p + q}(X, \mathcal{F})$, see
317	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-Leray}.
318	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images}
319	% we see that the sheaves $R^qf_*\mathcal{F}$ are quasi-coherent.
320	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}
321	% we see that $E_2^{p, q} = 0$ when $p > 0$.
322	% Hence the spectral sequence degenerates at $E_2$ and we win."
323	\begin{proof}
324	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, def:cech_complex}
325	  The question is local on \(S\), and the formation of both sides commutes with
326	  restriction to opens of \(S\), so we may assume \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) is
327	  affine. Because \(f\) is separated and quasi-compact, each finite intersection
328	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(f\) restricted to it is an affine
329	  morphism; by the affine acyclicity of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} the
330	  higher direct images of \(\mathcal{F}\) over each such intersection vanish, so the
331	  terms of the relative {\v C}ech complex carry no hidden higher cohomology.
332	
333	  Consider the {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral sequence of the cover
334	  \(\mathfrak{U}\), with \(E_2\)-page
335	  \(E_2^{p,q} = \check{H}^p(\mathfrak{U}, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}))\)
336	  converging to \(H^{p+q}(X, \mathcal{F})\). Since all intersections are affine,
337	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} forces the presheaf cohomology
338	  \(\underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F})\) to vanish on the intersections for \(q > 0\), so
339	  the spectral sequence collapses to its row \(q = 0\): the {\v C}ech cohomology of
340	  \(\mathcal{F}\) for the cover \(\mathfrak{U}\) computes the sheaf cohomology,
341	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})\) for all \(p\),
342	  and the same identification holds relatively, i.e.\ the cohomology sheaves of
343	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) compute the cohomology
344	  sheaves of \(Rf_*\mathcal{F}\).
345	
346	  It remains to identify these cohomology sheaves with \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\). Over
347	  the affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the Leray spectral sequence
348	  \(E_2^{p,q} = H^p(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F}) \Rightarrow H^{p+q}(X, \mathcal{F})\)
349	  has \(R^q f_*\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent, whence \(H^p(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F}) = 0\)
350	  for \(p > 0\) by Serre vanishing (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}). The Leray
351	  sequence therefore degenerates at \(E_2\), giving
352	  \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\). Combining with the previous
353	  paragraph, \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
354	  \cong R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules for all \(i \geq 0\).
355	\end{proof}
356	
357	\section{The unconditional higher direct image}
358	
359	\begin{definition}
360	[Unconditional higher direct image via {\v C}ech]
361	  \label{def:cech_higher_direct_image}
362	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechHigherDirectImage}
363	  \uses{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
364	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
365	  % \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}
366	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L843--854); the unconditional
367	  % packaging via the {\v C}ech complex is an Archon-original construction grounded
368	  % in this lemma and Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}.
369	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
370	  % Assume that $f$ is quasi-separated and quasi-compact.
371	  % Assume $S$ is affine.
372	  % For any quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{F}$
373	  % we have
374	  % $$
375	  % H^q(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})
376	  % $$
377	  % for all $q \in \mathbf{Z}$."
378	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
379	  \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}; unconditional
380	  {\v C}ech packaging is Archon-original.}
381	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) a
382	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Fix a finite affine open cover
383	  \(\mathfrak{U}\) of \(X\). The \emph{(unconditional) \(i\)-th higher direct
384	  image} is defined, for each \(i \geq 0\), as the \(i\)-th cohomology sheaf of the
385	  relative {\v C}ech complex,
386	  \[
387	    R^i f_*\mathcal{F} \;:=\;
388	      H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
389	      \in \operatorname{QCoh}(S).
390	  \]
391	  This requires \emph{no} hypothesis that the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
392	  have enough injectives: the right-hand side is the cohomology of an explicit
393	  complex of quasi-coherent sheaves. By
394	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology} the result is canonically isomorphic to
395	  the derived-functor higher direct image wherever the latter is defined, and in
396	  particular is independent of the chosen affine cover \(\mathfrak{U}\) up to
397	  canonical isomorphism: two affine covers admit a common affine refinement, and
398	  the induced refinement maps on {\v C}ech complexes are quasi-isomorphisms because
399	  both compute the same \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) by
400	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}. For \(i = 0\) one recovers the ordinary
401	  pushforward \(R^0 f_*\mathcal{F} = f_*\mathcal{F}\).
402	\end{definition}
403	
404	\section{Flat base change for the {\v C}ech higher direct images}
405	
406	\begin{lemma}
407	[Flat base change of $R^i f_*$ via {\v C}ech]
408	  \label{lem:cech_flat_base_change}
409	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cech_flatBaseChange}
410	  \uses{def:cech_higher_direct_image, lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
411	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KH (Lemma
412	  % \ref{lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology}, "Flat base change")
413	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L947--970).
414	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
415	  % $$
416	  % \xymatrix{
417	  % X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
418	  % S' \ar[r]^g & S
419	  % }
420	  % $$
421	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
422	  % with pullback $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$.
423	  % Assume that $g$ is flat and that $f$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
424	  % For any $i \geq 0$
425	  % \begin{enumerate}
426	  % \item the base change map of
427	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case}
428	  % is an isomorphism
429	  % $$
430	  % g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
431	  % $$
432	  % \item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
433	  % $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
434	  % \end{enumerate}"
435	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
436	  \ref{lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology} (Tag 02KH, ``Flat base change'').}
437	  Consider the cartesian square
438	  \[
439	    \begin{array}{ccc}
440	      X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
441	      {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
442	      S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
443	    \end{array}
444	  \]
445	  with \(f : X \to S\) separated and quasi-compact, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
446	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and
447	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). Assume \(g\) is flat. Then for every
448	  \(i \geq 0\) the canonical base-change map between the unconditional {\v C}ech
449	  higher direct images of Definition~\ref{def:cech_higher_direct_image} is an
450	  isomorphism
451	  \[
452	    g^*\bigl(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
453	      R^i f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr) = R^i f'_*\mathcal{F}'.
454	  \]
455	  Equivalently, when \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and
456	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat, the comparison map of
457	  \(B\)-modules \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \to H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\) is an
458	  isomorphism.
459	\end{lemma}
460	
461	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Having said this, part (1) follows from part (2). Namely,
462	% part (1) is local on $S'$ and hence we may assume $S$
463	% and $S'$ are affine. ...
464	% In case $X$ is separated, choose an affine open covering
465	% $\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \ldots \cup U_t$ and recall that
466	% $$
467	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F}),
468	% $$
469	% see
470	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}.
471	% If $\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \ldots \cup (U_t)_B$ we obtain
472	% by base change, then it is still the case that each $(U_i)_B$ is affine
473	% and that $X_B$ is separated. ...
474	% We have the following relation between the {\v C}ech complexes
475	% $$
476	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) =
477	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
478	% $$
479	% as follows from
480	% Lemma \ref{lemma-affine-base-change}.
481	% Since $A \to B$ is flat, the same thing remains true on taking cohomology."
482	\begin{proof}
483	  \uses{def:cech_higher_direct_image, lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
484	  The assertion that the base-change map is an isomorphism is local on \(S'\), so we
485	  may assume \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with
486	  \(A \to B\) flat. By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology} both
487	  \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) and \(R^i f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) are quasi-coherent, associated
488	  to the \(A\)-module \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F})\) and the \(B\)-module
489	  \(H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\) respectively; since pullback along \(g\) corresponds to
490	  \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules, it suffices to prove that the comparison map
491	  \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \to H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\) is an isomorphism.
492	
493	  Choose a finite affine open cover \(\mathfrak{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\);
494	  by separatedness all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) are affine. Base
495	  changing the cover yields an affine open cover \(\mathfrak{U}_B\) of the
496	  again-separated \(X' = X_B\), with each \((U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_B\) affine. By
497	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology} the two {\v C}ech complexes compute the
498	  respective cohomologies. The affine base change for the \(i = 0\) direct image
499	  identifies each term of the base-changed {\v C}ech complex with the original term
500	  tensored over \(A\) with \(B\), giving an isomorphism of cochain complexes
501	  \[
502	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
503	      \;\cong\;
504	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B.
505	  \]
506	  Because \(A \to B\) is flat, \(- \otimes_A B\) is exact and commutes with the
507	  formation of cohomology of a complex; applying \(H^i\) for any \(i \geq 0\) yields
508	  \[
509	    H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
510	      H^i(X', \mathcal{F}').
511	  \]
512	  Translating back to sheaves on \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) — using that the
513	  unconditional higher direct images are the quasi-coherent sheaves associated to
514	  these modules — gives the asserted isomorphism
515	  \(g^*(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}) \xrightarrow{\sim} R^i f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) for all
516	  \(i \geq 0\). The flatness of \(g\) (hence of \(g'\), by base change) is exactly
517	  what makes \(- \otimes_A B\) exact, and is therefore the precise hypothesis under
518	  which the {\v C}ech computation of Definition~\ref{def:cech_higher_direct_image}
519	  is compatible with base change.
520	\end{proof}
